description.value:
Four barrel-shaped clay cylinders inscribed with astronomical measurements and omens interpreting celestial phenomena. Created under command of Neo-Assyrian King Ashurbanipal, who sent royal explorer-scribes to gather information from across Mesopotamia. These tablets summarize myths and legends surrounding the Izdonarii, a non-human civilization that lives invisibly alongside humans, as well as mostly ineffective incantations and folk wisdom about resisting their powers.

LanguageAssyrian
AuthorUnnamed scribes
Date620 B.C.
Sanity Loss1D4/1D10
Cthulhu Mythos+3/+7 percentiles
Mythos Rating30
Study (weeks)12

Spells Contained

  • Augury
  • Identify Spirit
  • Imprison Mind
  • Telepathy
